title: Four Experiments — Making Copilot Honest
author: Peter Higgins — Rogue Wave Audio
purpose: Empirical verification of claims from Copilot's collective review paper package

Germany.name: Germany

three_way_decomposition.reason: Curl requires spatial derivatives in multiple independent directions. A single time series is a 1D path through ILR space. We have d/dt but not d/dx_i for i=1..D-1. The three-way split v = v_H + v_irr,⊥ + v_sol requires a FIELD (multiple trajectories covering the space), not a single trajectory.
three_way_decomposition.what_we_can_say: The remainder v_remainder has mean angular change 64.4° between consecutive steps, suggesting it contains both rotational and non-rotational components, but we cannot separate them from a single path.
three_way_decomposition.verdict: PARTIAL — two-way works perfectly; three-way requires multiple simultaneous trajectories (a velocity FIELD, not a single path)

Germany.honest_conclusion: Copilot's three-way decomposition is mathematically valid for a velocity FIELD on the simplex. For a single time series (which is what real data gives us), only the two-way decomposition (v_H + v_remainder) is computable. The three-way split would require an ensemble of trajectories sampling the simplex simultaneously.

Germany.name: Germany

correlation_with_chi.interpretation: Positive correlation means J tracks the non-gradient fraction (expected)

decomposition_attempt.interpretation: Drift=0.004466 (systematic bias present), oscillation rate=0.21 (low rotation)
decomposition_attempt.reason: Same as Experiment 2: formal irr/sol split requires a velocity field, not a single path

Germany.verdict: PARTIAL — Jensen residual J(t) is well-defined and computable. Correlation with χ(t) confirms it tracks the expected quantity. Formal decomposition into R_J^irr + R_J^sol faces the same single-path limitation as the three-way decomposition.

Japan.name: Japan

decomposition_attempt.interpretation: Drift=-0.001628 (no significant systematic bias), oscillation rate=0.17 (low rotation)
decomposition_attempt.reason: Same as Experiment 2: formal irr/sol split requires a velocity field, not a single path

Japan.verdict: PARTIAL — Jensen residual J(t) is well-defined and computable. Correlation with χ(t) confirms it tracks the expected quantity. Formal decomposition into R_J^irr + R_J^sol faces the same single-path limitation as the three-way decomposition.
